message:
fix(http): shut down transport on body stream completion and error

The body-stream unfold closure in send_parts() previously dropped the
transport implicitly when the stream ended or errored.  This could
leave TLS sessions in a half-closed state and leak OS sockets until
the async runtime's drop glue ran—potentially much later under heavy
concurrency.

Changes:
- Add BodyStreamState::shutdown_transport_best_effort() helper that
  calls transport.shutdown().await exactly once (guarded by a
  transport_closed flag to prevent double-shutdown)
- Call shutdown_transport_best_effort() in both the Ok(None) (stream
  exhausted) and Err (stream error) arms of the try_unfold closure
- Add transport_closed: bool field to BodyStreamState, initialized to
  false in new()

This ensures TLS close_notify is sent and the underlying TCP socket is
shut down promptly on every exit path, preventing file descriptor
exhaustion during sustained streaming workloads (e.g., long-running
SSE provider connections).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
